summary: No-ship: the gateway parser exemption is overbroad, and the gateway parse-error handler is not limited to body-parser errors.

findings.title: Global JSON parser skip exempts non-gateway /api/v1/llm* paths
findings.recommendation: Replace the startsWith predicate with an exact subtree check, e.g. req.path === '/api/v1/llm' || req.path.startsWith('/api/v1/llm/'). As written, /api/v1/llmfoo also bypasses the global parser; no such concrete router is mounted, but the /api/v1 404 handler is reachable without parsing, so malformed JSON on that non-gateway path will not produce the intended CONV-2 parse-error envelope.

findings.title: Gateway error handler shapes arbitrary 4xx-status errors, not just body-parser errors
findings.recommendation: Gate the 400 shaping on body-parser-specific properties/types, not only status >= 400 && status < 500; otherwise any future same-router 4xx error passed to next(err) is swallowed and reclassified as an invalid request body instead of next(err).
